Notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), if the aggregate of the amounts to be allotted to the States pursuant to such paragraphs for any fiscal year exceeds the total amount appropriated for such allotments under

children.text: In any year in which the total amount appropriated under

children.text: Monitoring the administration of the system

In a State in which the system is housed in a State agency, the State may use not more than 5 percent of any allotment under this subsection for the costs of monitoring the administration of the system required under
Technical assistance and American Indian consortium
In any case in which the total amount appropriated under

children.tail: for a fiscal year is more than $24,500,000, the Secretary shall—

children.text: use not more than 2 percent of the amount appropriated to provide technical assistance to eligible systems with respect to activities carried out under this part (consistent with requests by such systems for such assistance for the year); and
provide a grant in accordance with

children.tail: , and in an amount described in paragraph (2)(A)(i), to an American Indian consortium to provide protection and advocacy services.

children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary shall pay directly to any system in a State that complies with the provisions of this part the amount of the allotment made for the State under this section, unless the system specifies otherwise.

Any amount paid to a system under this part for a fiscal year and remaining unobligated at the end of such year shall remain available to such system for the next fiscal year, for the purposes for which such amount was paid.
